knownGaps: Article 17 grants forex accounts, but Foreign Exchange Code Article 6 still requires Central Bank authorization
Capital Market Decree (Décret 2018-1037) does not recognize SAFEs; Central Bank memo (Feb-2020) classifies them as debt
Commercial Code Article 154 enforces one-share-one-vote, blocking multi-class share structures
30% foreign-worker cap conflicts with talent strategy
notable: First African startup act; industry-led implementation model (Tunisian Startups association) outperformed pure government-led approaches.

knownGaps: Tax benefits require separate DGID verification (typically 45-60 days vs 30-day statutory limit)
Article 12 mentions 'titres convertibles' but lacks OHADA integration for SAFEs/convertible notes
BCEAO Instruction 2012-09 only recognises traditional debt and equity
20% foreign-worker quota constrains hiring
No mutual recognition with neighbouring Ivory Coast despite identical ECOWAS membership
notable: DER/FJ acts as both labeling authority and seed funder — unique single-window model.

labelingBody: National Council for Digital Innovation & Entrepreneurship (NCDIE)
knownGaps: Schedule 3 tax incentives not integrated into federal tax system; FIRS coordination ongoing
SEC Rules 2013 classify SAFEs as debt instruments; many founders use Cayman/Delaware parents to issue SAFEs
CAMA Section 124 enforces one-share-one-vote, blocking multi-class share structures
Pension fund rules (PenCom) restrict institutional capital from flowing to startups
notable: Largest GDP among enacted-act countries; CBN regulatory sandbox is most mature on the continent.

knownGaps: OHADA Uniform Act (2014) lacks provisions for complex cap tables and modern instruments
No legal recognition for SAFEs or convertible notes
Limited interface with banking and forex regulations
notable: Could harmonise immediately with Senegal and Ivory Coast under shared OHADA commercial code.

knownGaps: No automatic recognition of Senegal's startup label despite shared ECOWAS/OHADA/UEMOA membership
OHADA constraints on multi-class shares carry over
SAFE/convertible-note recognition unresolved
notable: Most recent enactment in francophone West Africa; strong candidate for OHADA-wide harmonisation pilot.

knownGaps: Proclamation does not authorize SAFEs or convertible notes
Implementation regulations still pending across most articles
Forex regime largely unchanged for labeled startups
notable: Tightest age limit (5 years) of any enacted act; broadest reform opportunity since regulations are still being drafted.

knownGaps: Foreign exchange controls remain a binding constraint
No public draft of securities provisions
notable: Already operating an Algerian Startup Fund; act would formalise the existing labelling already in use.

knownGaps: Significant Economic Presence Tax (SEPT) creates cross-border friction with Uganda/Tanzania
No mutual recognition with EAC partners despite Common Market Protocol Articles 13-14
notable: Largest startup ecosystem in EAC; passing this act would unlock EAC-wide passport pilot.

knownGaps: Securities Industry Act limits convertible instruments
Mandates pension fund 5% allocation to startups by 2026 — outpacing the act itself
notable: Already operates the Ghana–Rwanda Fintech Passport (Feb 2025), Africa's first cross-border mutual recognition system.

knownGaps: Forex restrictions on outbound capital
No SAFE/convertible-note recognition
notable: Strong fintech and B2B SaaS pipeline; potential anchor for Maghreb passport with Tunisia.

knownGaps: Mature regulatory sandbox not yet linked to a national startup act
Cap table and equity-compensation rules under company law remain rigid
notable: Counterpart to the Ghana–Rwanda Fintech Passport; widely seen as Africa's most agile regulator.

knownGaps: Section 12J expired (2021); no replacement vehicle for venture investing
Exchange control still requires SARB approval for offshore IP
Companies Act allows multi-class shares but practice is conservative
notable: Largest VC market in Africa by absolute deal volume; biggest single-country impact if the bill lands.

knownGaps: No mechanism to recognise Kenyan startup labelling under the Common Market Protocol
Capital Markets Authority does not register convertible instruments
notable: Could pioneer a Kenya–Uganda EAC passport pilot mirroring the Ghana–Rwanda model.

knownGaps: FRA does not recognise SAFEs/convertibles
Forex repatriation rules constrain dollar-denominated revenues
Companies Law caps non-voting shares
notable: Largest North African startup ecosystem by funding; strong fintech and logistics density.
